title: Request Context, Deadlines, Cancellation, and Cleanup · Runtime Contract: Cancel Cause Classification
summary: A request starts database and outbound work with background contexts, ignores disconnects, exhausts its entire deadline before cleanup, and leaks goroutines after middleware returns. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo cancel cause classification increment in deadline budget and cancellation propagation trace. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.
Allocate caller deadline across validation, database, outbound dependency, response, and cleanup reserves instead of stacking independent full timeouts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Cancel Cause Classification · Theory Cancel Cause Classification
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5902 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive a dependency budget while reserving time for cancellation cleanup; then return a five-hundred response and retain a low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish client cancellation, server deadline, dependency timeout, shutdown, overload, and internal failure for response and observability decisions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Cancel Cause Classification contract for the supplied data.
